data.description:
## Story S1: `test-run.sh` core launcher scaffold (Foundation / walking skeleton)

### Description
**What**: Create `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` with three subcommands — `start`, `status`, `kill`. `start` reads `commands.test_runner` (from epic 87e9-0bc8), launches it as a detached child via `setsid`, tees stdout+stderr to `<state_dir>/progress.log`, writes PID to `<state_dir>/pid`, and on child exit writes exit code to `<state_dir>/exit-code` via atomic write-then-rename, finally touching `<state_dir>/done`. `status` inspects the state dir and prints one of `running <pid>`, `done <exit-code>`, or `idle`. `kill` terminates the recorded PID via SIGTERM, grace from `commands.test_kill_grace_seconds` (default 30), then SIGKILL. No duplicate-launch guard yet (S3), no session isolation yet (S2).
**Why**: Every other story in this epic builds on this primitive. Without the scaffold, nothing downstream can land.
**Scope**:
- IN: script skeleton, three subcommands, atomic exit-code + done-marker ordering, SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL ladder, `commands.test_kill_grace_seconds` key
- OUT: session_id scoping (S2), duplicate-launch guard (S3), config validation (S4), legacy removal (S6), orchestrator docs (S5)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` exists with `start`, `status`, `kill` subcommands and is invocable via `.claude/scripts/dso test-run <cmd>`
  ← Satisfies: "test-run.sh exists and exposes three subcommands"
- When this story is complete, `start` writes `exit-code` via write-then-rename and creates `done` marker last (readers checking `done` before reading `exit-code` never observe partial content)
  ← Satisfies: "exit code to <state_dir>/exit-code via atomic write-then-rename, then touch <state_dir>/done last"
- When this story is complete, `kill` uses a configurable grace period from `commands.test_kill_grace_seconds` (default 30s), SIGTERM first, SIGKILL after the grace window
  ← Satisfies: "grace from commands.test_kill_grace_seconds (default 30s), then SIGKILL"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Reliability] `setsid` detaches the child from the controlling terminal — surviving orchestrator crashes is a feature, cleanup is Story S2's session-start hook responsibility
- [Testing] Unit tests must mock `commands.test_runner` invocation and verify state-file contents without running a real test suite
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO maintainer, test-run.sh core launcher scaffolds start/status/kill subcommands with atomic exit-code + done-marker ordering
